steps.title: Identify what 'regrowth' requires
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that environmental reforms “engendered a natural forest regrowth” by the mid-1980s. Regrowth means the forest cover must be increasing, not decreasing or stagnant. The evidence must show forest area expanding after the decline of the 1960s–1970s deforestation period.

steps.title: Match the expansion data
steps.content: Choice D shows forest cover increasing for all classes from 1979 to 2000, providing data that directly evidences the regrowth discussed in the passage. The other three document decline (A), a difference (B), or similarity (C)—none of which demonstrate expansion. That matches choice D — D is correct.
